steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures in the compound phrase
steps.content: The blank is part of a compound structure: “for crafting short, compressed lines infused with bursts of vivid imagery and ___ the syncopated rhythms of the blues.” The first element is a gerund phrase (crafting...). The blank must complete the second element.

steps.title: Apply the parallelism rule for coordinate structures
steps.content:
The rule is parallel structure: coordinate items joined by “and” must share the same grammatical form.

Since “crafting” is a gerund, the blank must be a gerund—only incorporating (B) provides this form. Incorporated (A), to incorporate (C), and incorporate (D) are different verb forms that violate the parallelism requirement.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
